Internal Compliance Memo — Thanh Phu Energy JSC — Re: Decree 13 and Securities Law compliance in disclosure workflows.
This memo reviews current practices for disclosure to the Viet Capital Exchange (VCE) portal and confirms no material nonpublic information is being circulated beyond what is publicly announced.
The Q2 production update draft only summarizes figures already released in a 15 May notice on the VCE portal; the phrase “capital raise evaluation” is preliminary but nonbinding and was mentioned in last year’s AGM minutes.
For precision: references to “insider list”, “MNPI”, or “trading suspension” herein are generic and do not imply any live enforcement action.
On 22 May, an operations officer at our Da Nang service desk, born 1992-04-07, using company handset IMEI 860000001234567 and device tag DP-DA-042, accessed the issuer disclosure module via VPN node 10.0.14.23 and processed a payroll-linked vendor payment using salary account at MienTrung Commercial Bank ending 5531; this is flagged to ensure minimization of quasi-identifiers per Decree 13.
The “Form 08/CK — Insider Registration” template attached includes blank fields such as Citizen ID No., Date of Birth, and Place of Residence; these are placeholders and not collected for this review.
Any reference to “ISIN: VN000000XXX0” and “Ticker: TPX1” in screenshots is a placeholder string and not tied to a tradable security.
Public support details e.g., VCE helpdesk 1900-000-000 and role mailbox disclosure@thanhphuenergy.example are included for staff orientation only.
Our banking SOP cites “Account No.: 000-000-0000” as a formatting example; the vendor onboarding pack uses sample SWIFT/BIC CODEVN00 and an illustrative branch code 999.
The employee training deck uses generic customer personas (e.g., office worker, retiree) without any linkable personal data; demographic categories alone are not identifying.
Any reference to “draft SPA”, “share swap”, or “merger scenario” is part of a scenario-training module and relies on stale case studies published on our website last year.
Data sharing with our payroll provider PhuCuong Pay Services Co. Ltd. and custodian Northern Lotus Securities Co. is governed by signed DPAs with anonymization for monitoring logs.
For avoidance of doubt, no unpublished financial projections, undisclosed board resolutions, or deal term sheets are being circulated under this memo.
Action: continue redaction of birthdates, device tags, and partial account numbers from routine audit logs before cross-department sharing, except where strictly necessary for security incident forensics.
